name: French Guiana (France)

colors.symbolism: the dense Amazonian rainforest covering most of the territory
the territory's mineral wealth — especially gold — and the warmth of the equatorial climate
the central star — the blood of the people and the labour movement of the territory

description: A field divided diagonally from the upper hoist to the lower fly, with the upper-fly triangle green and the lower-hoist triangle yellow, charged at the centre with a red five-pointed star.
history: Adopted by the General Council (Conseil général) of French Guiana on 29 January 2010, the flag has unofficial status — French Guiana is an overseas region and department of France, and the official flag remains the French tricolour. The design has been used since the 1960s by independence movements and is widely associated with Guianese identity, but it has not been formally adopted by the regional council.

features.symbolism: the diagonal split represents the union of the territory's lush rainforests (green) with its mineral wealth, especially the historic gold-mining economy (yellow)
A red five-pointed star representing the people of French Guiana and their aspirations; common in flags of left-leaning regional movements in the 20th century.
